2605.02520.title: Benchmarking Retrieval Strategies for Biomedical Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Controlled Empirical Study
2605.02520.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers a well-established path to grounding large language model (LLM) outputs in external knowledge, yet the question of which retrieval strategy works best in a high-stakes domain such as biomedicine has not received the controlled, multi-metric treatment it deserves. This paper presents a systematic empirical comparison of five retrieval strategies -- Dense Vector Search, Hybrid BM25 + Dense retrieval, Cross-Encoder Reranking, Multi-Query Expansion, and Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) -- within a biomedical question-answering RAG pipeline. All strategies share a fixed generation model (GPT-4o-mini), a common vector store (ChromaDB), and OpenAI's text-embedding-3-small embeddings, ensuring that observed differences are attributable to retrieval alone. Evaluation is conducted on 250 question-answer pairs drawn from a preprocessed subset of the BioASQ benchmark (rag-mini-bioasq) using four DeepEval metrics: contextual precision, contextual recall, faithfulness, and answer relevancy, each reported with 95% confidence intervals. A no-context ablation is included as a lower bound. Cross-Encoder Reranking achieves the best composite score (0.827) and highest contextual precision (0.852), confirming that query-document interaction yields measurable retrieval gains. Multi-Query Expansion, despite its recall-oriented design, produces the weakest contextual precision (0.671), suggesting naive query diversification introduces retrieval noise. MMR sacrifices answer relevancy for diversity, while the Dense baseline (composite 0.822) falls within 0.005 points of the top strategy. All RAG conditions dramatically outperform the no-context ablation on answer relevancy (0.658-0.701 vs. 0.287), confirming the practical value of retrieval. The full pipeline, hyperparameters, and evaluation code are publicly available.
2605.02520.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）提供了一條成熟的路徑，將大型語言模型（LLM）的輸出與外部知識相結合，但在生物醫學等高風險領域中，哪種檢索策略效果最佳的問題尚未得到應有的控制和多指標處理。本文對五種檢索策略進行了系統的實證比較——密集向量搜索、混合BM25 + 密集檢索、交叉編碼重排序、多查詢擴展和最大邊際相關性（MMR）——在生物醫學問答RAG管道中。所有策略共享一個固定的生成模型（GPT-4o-mini）、一個共同的向量存儲（ChromaDB）和OpenAI的text-embedding-3-small嵌入，確保觀察到的差異僅可歸因於檢索。評估基於從BioASQ基準（rag-mini-bioasq）預處理子集中提取的250個問答對，使用四個DeepEval指標：上下文精確度、上下文召回率、忠實度和答案相關性，每個指標均報告95%的置信區間。還包括一個無上下文的消融作為下限。交叉編碼重排序達到了最佳的綜合得分（0.827）和最高的上下文精確度（0.852），確認查詢-文檔交互帶來了可測量的檢索增益。儘管多查詢擴展的設計以召回為導向，但其產生的上下文精確度最弱（0.671），這表明天真的查詢多樣化引入了檢索噪音。MMR為了多樣性而犧牲了答案相關性，而密集基線（綜合得分0.822）與最佳策略相差僅0.005分。所有RAG條件在答案相關性上都顯著超過無上下文消融（0.658-0.701對比0.287），確認了檢索的實際價值。完整的管道、超參數和評估代碼均已公開。
2605.02520.author: Devi Prasad Bal et.al.
